addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

10	# NGINX Gateway Fabric
11	
12	NGINX Gateway Fabric is an open-source project that provides an implementation of
13	the [Gateway API](https://gateway-api.sigs.k8s.io/) using [NGINX](https://nginx.org/) as the data plane. The goal of
14	this project is to implement the core Gateway APIs -- `Gateway`, `GatewayClass`, `HTTPRoute`, `GRPCRoute`, `TCPRoute`, `TLSRoute`,
15	and `UDPRoute` -- to configure an HTTP or TCP/UDP load balancer, reverse-proxy, or API gateway for applications running
16	on Kubernetes.
17	
18	For a list of supported Gateway API resources and features, see
19	the [Gateway API Compatibility](https://docs.nginx.com/nginx-gateway-fabric/overview/gateway-api-compatibility/) doc.
20	
21	Learn about our [design principles](/docs/developer/design-principles.md) and [architecture](https://docs.nginx.com/nginx-gateway-fabric/overview/gateway-architecture/).
22	
23	NGINX Gateway Fabric uses [NGINX Agent](https://github.com/nginx/agent) to configure NGINX.
24	
25	## Getting Started
26	
27	1. [Get started using a kind cluster](https://docs.nginx.com/nginx-gateway-fabric/get-started/).
28	2. [Install](https://docs.nginx.com/nginx-gateway-fabric/install/) NGINX Gateway Fabric.
29	3. Deploy various [examples](examples).
30	4. Follow instructions for common use cases such as [routing](https://docs.nginx.com/nginx-gateway-fabric/traffic-management/) and [securing](https://docs.nginx.com/nginx-gateway-fabric/traffic-security/) traffic, or [monitoring](https://docs.nginx.com/nginx-gateway-fabric//monitoring/) your cluster.
31	
32	You can find the comprehensive NGINX Gateway Fabric user documentation on the [NGINX Documentation](https://docs.nginx.com/nginx-gateway-fabric/) website.
33	
34	## NGINX Gateway Fabric Releases
35	
36	We publish NGINX Gateway Fabric releases on GitHub. See
37	our [releases page](https://github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/releases).
38	
39	The latest release is [2.6.5](https://github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/releases/tag/v2.6.5).
40

53	### Versioning

96	## SBOM (Software Bill of Materials)
97	
98	We generate SBOMs for the binaries and the Docker image.
99	
100	### Binaries
101	
102	The SBOMs for the binaries are available in the releases page. The SBOMs are generated
103	using [syft](https://github.com/anchore/syft) and are available in SPDX format.
104	
105	### Docker Images
106	
107	The SBOM for the Docker image is available in
108	the [GitHub Container](https://github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/pkgs/container/nginx-gateway-fabric)
109	repository. The SBOM is generated using [syft](https://github.com/anchore/syft) and stored as an attestation in the
110	image manifest.
111	
112	For example to retrieve the SBOM for `linux/amd64` and analyze it using [grype](https://github.com/anchore/grype) you
113	can run the following command:
114	
115	```shell
116	docker buildx imagetools inspect ghcr.io/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric:edge --format '{{ json (index .SBOM "linux/amd64").SPDX }}' | grype
117	```

# NGINX Gateway Fabric

NGINX Gateway Fabric is an open-source project that provides an implementation of
the [Gateway API](https://gateway-api.sigs.k8s.io/) using [NGINX](https://nginx.org/) as the data plane. The goal of
this project is to implement the core Gateway APIs -- `Gateway`, `GatewayClass`, `HTTPRoute`, `GRPCRoute`, `TCPRoute`, `TLSRoute`,
and `UDPRoute` -- to configure an HTTP or TCP/UDP load balancer, reverse-proxy, or API gateway for applications running
on Kubernetes.

For a list of supported Gateway API resources and features, see
the [Gateway API Compatibility](https://docs.nginx.com/nginx-gateway-fabric/overview/gateway-api-compatibility/) doc.

Learn about our [design principles](/docs/developer/design-principles.md) and [architecture](https://docs.nginx.com/nginx-gateway-fabric/overview/gateway-architecture/).

NGINX Gateway Fabric uses [NGINX Agent](https://github.com/nginx/agent) to configure NGINX.

## Getting Started

1. [Get started using a kind cluster](https://docs.nginx.com/nginx-gateway-fabric/get-started/).
2. [Install](https://docs.nginx.com/nginx-gateway-fabric/install/) NGINX Gateway Fabric.
3. Deploy various [examples](examples).
4. Follow instructions for common use cases such as [routing](https://docs.nginx.com/nginx-gateway-fabric/traffic-management/) and [securing](https://docs.nginx.com/nginx-gateway-fabric/traffic-security/) traffic, or [monitoring](https://docs.nginx.com/nginx-gateway-fabric//monitoring/) your cluster.

You can find the comprehensive NGINX Gateway Fabric user documentation on the [NGINX Documentation](https://docs.nginx.com/nginx-gateway-fabric/) website.

## NGINX Gateway Fabric Releases

We publish NGINX Gateway Fabric releases on GitHub. See
our [releases page](https://github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/releases).

The latest release is [2.6.5](https://github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/releases/tag/v2.6.5).

The edge version is useful for experimenting with new features that are not yet published in a release. To use, choose
the _edge_ version built from the [latest commit](https://github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/commits/main)
from the main branch.

The table below summarizes the options regarding the images, manifests, documentation and examples and gives your links
to the correct versions:

| Version | Description | Installation Manifests | Documentation and Examples |
| ------- | ----------- | ---------------------- | -------------------------- |
| Latest release | For production use | [Manifests](https://github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/tree/v2.6.5/deploy). | [Documentation](https://docs.nginx.com/nginx-gateway-fabric). [Examples](https://github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/tree/v2.6.5/examples). |
| Edge | For experimental use and latest features | [Manifests](https://github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/tree/main/deploy). | [Examples](https://github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/tree/main/examples). |

### Versioning

NGF uses semantic versioning for its releases. For more information, see https://semver.org.

> Major version zero `(0.Y.Z)` is reserved for development, anything MAY change at any time. The public API is not stable.

### Release Planning and Development

The features that will go into the next release are reflected in the
corresponding [milestone](https://github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/milestones). Refer to
the [Issue Lifecycle](ISSUE_LIFECYCLE.md) document for information on issue creation and assignment to releases.

## Technical Specifications

The following table lists the software versions NGINX Gateway Fabric supports. Only the latest patch release for each minor version is shown.

| NGINX Gateway Fabric | Gateway API | Kubernetes | NGINX OSS | NGINX Plus | NGINX Agent | F5 WAF for NGINX |

## SBOM (Software Bill of Materials)

We generate SBOMs for the binaries and the Docker image.

### Binaries

The SBOMs for the binaries are available in the releases page. The SBOMs are generated
using [syft](https://github.com/anchore/syft) and are available in SPDX format.

### Docker Images

The SBOM for the Docker image is available in
the [GitHub Container](https://github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/pkgs/container/nginx-gateway-fabric)
repository. The SBOM is generated using [syft](https://github.com/anchore/syft) and stored as an attestation in the
image manifest.

For example to retrieve the SBOM for `linux/amd64` and analyze it using [grype](https://github.com/anchore/grype) you
can run the following command:

```shell
docker buildx imagetools inspect ghcr.io/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric:edge --format '{{ json (index .SBOM "linux/amd64").SPDX }}' | grype
```

## Troubleshooting

For troubleshooting help, see the [Troubleshooting](https://docs.nginx.com/nginx-gateway-fabric/troubleshooting/) document.

## Contacts

We’d like to hear your feedback! If you experience issues with our Gateway Controller, please [open a bug][bug] in
GitHub. If you have any suggestions or enhancement requests, please [open an idea][idea] on GitHub discussions. You can
contact us directly on the [NGINX Community Forum][forum].

[bug]: https://github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/issues/new?assignees=&labels=&projects=&template=bug_report.md&title=
[idea]: https://github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/discussions/categories/ideas
[forum]: https://community.nginx.org/c/projects/nginx-gateway-fabric/25

## Community Meetings

Every Tuesday, alternating time zones at 9:30AM Pacific / 5:30PM GMT or 12:00PM GMT.

For the meeting link, updates, agenda, and meeting notes, check the link below:

[NGINX Gateway Fabric Community Meeting](https://github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/discussions/1472)

If you have a use case for NGINX Gateway Fabric that the project can't quite meet yet, bugs, problems, success stories, or just want to be more involved with the project, come by and say hi!

## Contributing

Please read our [Contributing guide](CONTRIBUTING.md) if you'd like to contribute to the project.

## Support and NGINX Plus

If your team needs dedicated support for NGINX Gateway Fabric in your environment, or you would like to leverage our [advanced NGINX Plus features](https://docs.nginx.com/nginx-gateway-fabric/overview/nginx-plus/), you can reach out [here](https://www.f5.com/content/f5-com/en_us/products/get-f5).

To try NGINX Gateway Fabric with NGINX Plus, you can start your free [30-day trial](https://www.f5.com/trials), then follow the [installation guide](https://docs.nginx.com/nginx-gateway-fabric/install/helm/) for installing with NGINX Plus.

file.content:
# Design Principles

The aim of the NGINX Gateway Fabric is to become a fundamental infrastructure component within a Kubernetes cluster,
serving as both an ingress and egress point for traffic directed towards the services (applications) running within or
outside the cluster. Leveraging NGINX as a data plane technology, it harnesses the well-established reputation of NGINX
as an open-source project widely recognized for its role as a web server, proxy, load balancer, and content cache. NGINX
is renowned for its stability, high performance, security, and rich feature set, positioning it as a critical
infrastructure tool. Notably, once properly configured and operational, NGINX requires minimal attention, making it
reliable and steady software.

The NGINX Gateway Fabric aims to embody the same qualities as NGINX and become familiar, trustworthy and reliable
software. The principles outlined below serve as a guide for engineering the NGINX Gateway Fabric with the intention
of achieving this goal.

## Security

We are security first. We prioritize security from the outset, thoroughly evaluating each design and feature with a
focus on security. We proactively identify and safeguard assets at the early stages of our processes, ensuring their
protection throughout the development lifecycle. We adhere to best practices for secure design, including proper
authentication, authorization, and encryption mechanisms.

## Availability

As a critical infrastructure component, we must be highly available. We design and review features with redundancy and
fault tolerance in mind. We regularly test the NGINX Gateway Fabric's availability by simulating failure scenarios
and conducting load testing. We work to identify potential weaknesses and bottlenecks, and address them to ensure high
availability under various conditions.

## Performance

We must be highly performant and lightweight. We fine-tune the NGINX configuration to maximize performance without
requiring custom configuration. We strive to minimize our memory and CPU footprint, enabling efficient resource
allocation and reducing unnecessary processing overhead. We use profiling tools on our code to identify bottlenecks and
improve performance.

## Resilience

We design with resilience in mind. This includes gracefully handling failures, such as Pod restarts or network
interruptions, as well as leveraging Kubernetes features like health checks, readiness probes, and container restart
policies.

## Observability

We provide comprehensive logging, metrics, and tracing capabilities to gain insights into our behavior and performance.
We prioritize Kubernetes-native observability tools like Prometheus, Grafana, and distributed tracing systems to help
users monitor the health of NGINX Gateway Fabric and to assist in diagnosing issues.

## Ease of Use

NGINX Gateway Fabric must be easy and intuitive to use. This means that it should be easy to install, easy to
configure, and easy to monitor. Its defaults should be sane and should lead to "out-of-box" success. The documentation
should be clear and provide meaningful examples that customer's can use to inform their deployments and configurations.

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the framework. Let me compile a structured report of the findings.

## STRUCTURED FRAMEPACK ANALYSIS: `internal/framework/` 

### OVERVIEW
The `internal/framework/` directory implements a reusable in-house framework built on top of `controller-runtime`. It provides controller abstractions, event processing machinery, resource filtering/predicates, and specialized subsystems for WAF (Web Application Firewall) bundle management and file operations.

---

## SUBPACKAGE ANALYSIS

### **1. controller/ - Controller Runtime Abstractions**
**Purpose:** Abstracts controller-runtime's manager/controller builder patterns with standardized filtering, indexing, and event dispatch.

**Key Files:**
- `register.go` - Main controller registration API
- `reconciler.go` - Core reconciliation logic  
- `getter.go` - Resource getter interface abstraction
- `resource.go` - Resource naming utilities (truncation/hashing)
- `labels.go` - Standard labels for NGINX resources

**Key Types/Interfaces:**
- `Reconciler` - Implements `reconcile.Reconciler`, sends `UpsertEvent`/`DeleteEvent` to event channel
- `ReconcilerConfig` - Configures reconciler with `Getter`, `ObjectType`, event channel, filters
- `NamespacedNameFilterFunc` - Filters resources before processing
- `Register()` - Main function; registers controllers with manager, configures field indices
- `Option` functional options: `WithNamespacedNameFilter()`, `WithK8sPredicate()`, `WithFieldIndices()`, `WithOnlyMetadata()`
- `AddIndex()` - Adds field indices to manager's FieldIndexer

**Subdirectories:**
- **filter/** - `CreateSingleResourceFilter()` for single-resource controllers
- **predicate/** - Event filtering predicates:
  - `GatewayClassPredicate` - Filters by controller name
  - `SecretNamePredicate` - Filters by namespace/name
  - `ServiceChangedPredicate` - Detects port/targetPort changes
  - `AnnotationPredicate` - Filters by annotation presence/value changes
  - `RestartDeploymentAnnotationPredicate` - Ignores rolling restart events
  - `NginxLabelPredicate` - Filters by nginx labels
  - `PLMStatusChangedPredicate` - Policy lifecycle manager status changes
- **index/** - Field indexers:
  - `CreateEndpointSliceFieldIndices()` - Indexes EndpointSlices by service name
  - `PodIPIndexFunc()` - Indexes Pods by IP
  - `ServiceNameIndexFunc()` - Extracts service name from EndpointSlice labels
- **cache/transform.go** - Cache transforms:
  - `TransformGatewayClass()` - Strips non-matching GatewayClasses
  - `TransformSecret()` - Filters secret data keys
  - `TransformConfigMap()` - Filters ConfigMap data keys

---

### **2. events/ - Event Processing Machinery**  
**Purpose:** Implements batching event loop for efficient NGINX config reloads, with initial cluster state preparation.

**Key Files:**
- `loop.go` - Main event loop with double-buffering
- `handler.go` - EventHandler interface for event processing
- `event.go` - Event type definitions
- `first_eventbatch_preparer.go` - Initial cluster state bootstrap

**Key Types/Interfaces:**
- `EventLoop` - Main event loop; batches events, uses double-buffering to minimize NGINX reloads

Based on my thorough exploration of the `internal/controller/` directory, I now have a comprehensive understanding of the NGINX Gateway Fabric controller architecture. Let me provide you with the structured report:

## NGINX Gateway Fabric Controller Architecture Report

### **Overall Architecture**

The NGINX Gateway Fabric controller implements a sophisticated event-driven reconciliation system that translates Kubernetes Gateway API resources into NGINX configuration files and pushes them to NGINX agents running in data plane pods. The system follows a classic controller pattern with multiple specialized components handling different aspects of the reconciliation lifecycle.

---

### **Core Data Flow**

**Event Processing Pipeline:**
1. **Event Generation** → Kubernetes resource changes are detected by controller-runtime controllers
2. **Event Channel** → Events flow through a unified `eventCh` channel to the EventHandler
3. **State Capture** → ChangeProcessor captures changes in object stores
4. **Graph Building** → Process() builds a Graph representation of Gateway API resources
5. **Config Generation** → Generator converts Graph to NGINX configuration files
6. **Config Push** → NginxUpdater sends configs via gRPC to NGINX Agent
7. **Status Updates** → StatusUpdater updates Kubernetes resource statuses

**Central Reconciliation Loop:** The `state.ChangeProcessor` is the core processor that transforms captured changes into a `graph.Graph` representation. It maintains cluster state in typed object stores and rebuilds the graph when changes occur.

---

### **Subpackage Analysis**

#### **1. internal/controller (root)**
**Purpose:** Main controller orchestration and manager initialization.

**Key Files:**
- `/internal/controller/manager.go` - Controller lifecycle management and component wiring

- `createManager()` - Creates controller-runtime manager with cache configuration

## COMPREHENSIVE STRUCTURED REPORT

---

### AREA 1: CLI ENTRANCEPOINTS IN `/cmd/gateway/`

**File: `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/nginxinc-nginx-gateway-fabric/cmd/gateway/commands.go`**

#### Subcommands Available:
1. **controller** - Main NGINX Gateway Fabric control plane
2. **generate-certs** - Generate self-signed TLS certificates for control plane ↔ data plane communication
3. **initialize** - Init container to write initial configuration files
4. **sleep** - Sleep command for lifecycle hooks (temporary until Kubernetes supports sleep in preStop)
5. **endpoint-picker** - Gateway API Inference Extension endpoint picker shim server

---

#### **`controller` Command** (File: commands.go, lines 85-634)
**Purpose:** Run the NGINX Gateway Fabric control plane

**Key Flags:**
- `--gateway-ctlr-name` (required) - Controller name in format `gateway.nginx.org/PATH`
- `--gatewayclass` (required) - Name of GatewayClass resource
- `--config` / `-c` - Name of NginxGateway resource for dynamic configuration
- `--service` - Name of Service fronting the NGINX Gateway Fabric Pod
- `--nginx-plus` - Enable NGINX Plus
- `--agent-tls-secret` - TLS secret for NGINX Agent communication
- `--nginx-one-dataplane-key-secret` - NGINX One Console dataplane key secret
- `--nginx-one-telemetry-endpoint-host/port` - NGINX One Console telemetry endpoint
- `--nginx-one-tls-skip-verify` - Disable TLS verification for NGINX One
- `--metrics-disable/port/secure-serving` - Metrics server configuration (default: port 9113)
- `--health-disable/port` - Health probe server configuration (default: port 8081)
- `--leader-election-disable/lock-name` - Leader election settings
- `--product-telemetry-disable` - Disable product telemetry collection
- `--gateway-api-experimental-features` - Enable Gateway API experimental features
- `--gateway-api-inference-extension` - Enable Gateway API Inference Extension support
- `--snippets` / `--snippets-filters` (deprecated) - Enable NGINX configuration snippets
- `--nginx-scc` - SecurityContextConstraints for OpenShift
- `--watch-namespaces` - Comma-separated list of namespaces to watch
- `--server-tls-domain` - Domain suffix for server TLS certificate (default: "svc")
- `--plm-storage-*` - PLM S3-compatible storage configuration for WAF policies
- `--usage-report-*` - NGINX Plus usage reporting configuration

---

#### **`generate-certs` Command** (File: commands.go, lines 719-832; certs.go)
**Purpose:** Generate self-signed certificates for securing control plane ↔ data plane communication

**Key Functions (certs.go):**
- `generateCertificates()` - Creates CA, server, and client certificates (3-year validity)
- `generateCA()` - Creates root CA certificate
- `generateCert()` - Creates signed certificates using CA
- `createSecrets()` - Creates/secrets: `server-tls` and `agent-tls` Kubernetes secrets

**Key Flags:**
- `--server-tls-secret` - Name of server TLS secret (default: `server-tls`)
- `--agent-tls-secret` - Name of agent TLS secret (default: `agent-tls`)
- `--service` - Name of Service fronting the Pod
- `--cluster-domain` - DNS domain of Kubernetes cluster (default: `cluster.local`)
- `--server-tls-domain` - Domain suffix for server TLS SAN (default: `svc`)
- `--overwrite` - Overwrite existing certificates

---

#### **`initialize` Command** (File: commands.go, lines 834-917; initialize.go)
**Purpose:** Write initial configuration files (runs in init container)

**Key Functions (initialize.go):**
- `initialize()` - Copies files and generates deployment context for NGINX Plus
- `copyFile()` - Copies source files to destination directories

**Key Flags:**
- `--source` - Source files to copy
- `--destination` - Destination directories (paired with source array)
- `--nginx-plus` - Enable NGINX Plus mode (generates deployment context file)

---

#### **`sleep` Command** (File: commands.go, lines 924-948)
**Purpose:** Sleep for specified duration (used in lifecycle hooks)

**Key Flags:**
- `--duration` - Sleep duration (default: 30s, accepts Go duration format)

---

#### **`endpoint-picker` Command** (File: commands.go, lines 950-969; endpoint_picker.go)
**Purpose:** Shim server for NGINX ↔ Gateway API Inference Extension Endpoint Picker communication

**Key Functions (endpoint_picker.go):**
- `endpointPickerServer()` - Starts HTTP server on port 9119 (GoShimPort)
- `createEndpointPickerHandler()` - Creates HTTP handler that forwards requests to EndpointPicker via gRPC External Processing API
- `realExtProcClientFactory()` - Creates gRPC client with TLS/insecure options

**Key Flags:**
- `--endpoint-picker-disable-tls` - Disable TLS when connecting to EndpointPicker
- `--endpoint-picker-tls-skip-verify` - Skip server certificate verification (default: true)

---

#### **Validation Functions** (File: validation.go, validating_types.go)
**Purpose:** Admission webhook validation and CLI flag validation

**Key Validators:**
- `validateGatewayControllerName()` - Validates controller name format `gateway.nginx.org/PATH`
- `validateResourceName()` - Validates DNS1123 subdomain format
- `validateNamespacedResourceName()` - Validates `namespace/name` format
- `validateIP()`, `validateEndpoint()`, `validatePort()` - Network address validation
- `validateURL()` - URL scheme validation (http/https only)
- `ensureNoPortCollisions()` - Prevents duplicate port assignments

**Validation Types (validating_types.go):**
- `stringValidatingValue` - String flag with custom validator
- `stringSliceValidatingValue` - Comma-separated string slice flag
- `intValidatingValue` - Integer flag with range validation

---

### AREA 2: API TYPES

#### **`apis/v1alpha1`** (Go package: `gateway.nginx.org/v1alpha1`)

**File: `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/nginxinc-nginx-gateway-fabric/apis/v1alpha1/register.go`**

**CRD Types Defined (9 types):**

1. **`NginxGateway`** - Dynamic configuration for NGINX Gateway Fabric control plane
   - Purpose: Top-level configuration resource with logging settings
   - Key field: `logging.level` (info/debug/error)

2. **`ClientSettingsPolicy`** (Inherited Attached Policy)
   - Purpose: Configure client ↔ NGINX Gateway Fabric connection behavior
   - Key settings: Body size limits, keep-alive configuration

3. **`ProxySettingsPolicy`** (Inherited Attached Policy)
   - Purpose: Configure NGINX Gateway Fabric ↔ upstream connection behavior
   - Key settings: Response buffering, timeouts

4. **`UpstreamSettingsPolicy`** (Direct Attached Policy)
   - Purpose: Configure NGINX upstream server behavior for specific Services
   - Key settings: Load balancing methods, keep-alive, zone size
   - Supports: 12 load balancing algorithms (round_robin, least_conn, ip_hash, hash, hash consistent, random, random two, random two least_conn, random two least_time=header, random two least_time=last_byte, least_time header/last_byte with/without inflight)

5. **`SnippetsFilter`** - Filter for HTTPRoute/GRPCRoute
   - Purpose: Insert NGINX configuration snippets into HTTPRoute/GRPCRoute contexts
   - Allowed contexts: main, http, http.server, http.server.location

6. **`SnippetsPolicy`** (Direct Attached Policy)
   - Purpose: Insert NGINX configuration snippets into Gateway contexts
   - Similar to SnippetsFilter but targets Gateway resources

7. **`AuthenticationFilter`** - Filter for HTTPRoute/GRPCRoute
   - Purpose: Configure request authentication
   - Auth types: Basic, JWT (NGINX Plus), OIDC (NGINX Plus)
   - Includes authorization rules for token claim validation

8. **`RateLimitPolicy`** (Inherited Attached Policy)
   - Purpose: Local rate limiting for requests
   - Key settings: Rate (r/s or r/m), burst, delay, zone size

9. **`WAFPolicy`** (Inherited Attached Policy)
   - Purpose: Configure F5 WAF for NGINX (App Protect)
   - Sources: HTTP (direct URL), NIM (NGINX Instance Manager), N1C (NGINX One Console), PLM (Policy Lifecycle Manager with S3 storage)

# Development Quickstart

This guide will assist you in setting up your development environment for NGINX Gateway Fabric, covering the steps
to build, install, and execute tasks necessary for submitting pull requests. By following this guide, you'll have a
fully prepared development environment that allows you to contribute to the project effectively.

<!-- START doctoc generated TOC please keep comment here to allow auto update -->
<!-- DON'T EDIT THIS SECTION, INSTEAD RE-RUN doctoc TO UPDATE -->
## Table of Contents

- [Setup Your Development Environment](#setup-your-development-environment)
- [Build the Binary and Images](#build-the-binary-and-images)
  - [Setting GOARCH](#setting-goarch)
  - [Build the Binary](#build-the-binary)
  - [Build the Images](#build-the-images)
  - [Build the Images with NGINX Plus](#build-the-images-with-nginx-plus)
- [Deploy on Kind](#deploy-on-kind)
  - [Run Examples](#run-examples)
- [Run the Unit Tests](#run-the-unit-tests)
- [Gateway API Conformance Testing](#gateway-api-conformance-testing)
- [Run the Linter](#run-the-linter)
- [Run the Helm Linter](#run-the-helm-linter)
- [Update all the generated files](#update-all-the-generated-files)

<!-- END doctoc generated TOC please keep comment here to allow auto update -->

## Setup Your Development Environment

Follow these steps to set up your development environment.

1. Install:

   - [Go](https://golang.org/doc/install) v1.21.0+
   - [Docker](https://docs.docker.com/get-docker/) v18.09+
   - [Kubectl](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/tools/#kubectl)
   - [Kind](https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/docs/user/quick-start/)
   - [Helm](https://helm.sh/docs/intro/quickstart/#install-helm)
   - [git](https://git-scm.com/)
   - [GNU Make](https://www.gnu.org/software/software.html)
   - [yq](https://github.com/mikefarah/yq/#install)
   - [fieldalignment](https://pkg.go.dev/golang.org/x/tools/go/analysis/passes/fieldalignment):

   ```shell
   go install golang.org/x/tools/go/analysis/passes/fieldalignment/cmd/fieldalignment@latest
   ```

   - [pre-commit](https://pre-commit.com/#install):

   ```shell
   brew install pre-commit
   ```

2. [Fork the project repository](https://github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/fork)
3. Clone your repository with ssh, and install the project dependencies:

   ```shell
   git clone git@github.com:<YOUR-USERNAME>/nginx-gateway-fabric.git
   cd nginx-gateway-fabric
   ```

   and then run

   ```shell
   pre-commit install
   ```

   in the project root directory to install the git hooks.

   ```makefile
   make deps
   ```

4. Finally, add the original project repository as the remote upstream:

   ```shell
   git remote add upstream git@github.com:nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric.git
   ```


## Build the Binary and Images

### Setting GOARCH

The [Makefile](/Makefile) uses the GOARCH variable to build the binary and container images. The default value of GOARCH is `amd64`.

If you are deploying NGINX Gateway Fabric on a kind cluster, and the architecture of your machine is not `amd64`, you will want to set the GOARCH variable to the architecture of your local machine. You can find the value of GOARCH by running `go env`. Export the GOARCH variable in your `~/.zshrc` or `~/.bashrc`.

```shell
echo "export GOARCH=< Your architecture (e.g. arm64 or amd64) >" >> ~/.bashrc
source ~/.bashrc
```

or for zsh:

```shell
echo "export GOARCH=< Your architecture (e.g. arm64 or amd64) >" >> ~/.zshrc
source ~/.zshrc
```

### Build the Binary

To build the binary, run the make build command from the project's root directory:

```makefile
make GOARCH=$GOARCH build
```

This command will build the binary and output it to the `/build/.out` directory.

### Build the Images

To build the NGINX Gateway Fabric and NGINX container images from source run the following make command:

```makefile
make GOARCH=$GOARCH TAG=$(whoami) build-images
```

This will build the docker images `nginx-gateway-fabric:<your-user>` and `nginx-gateway-fabric/nginx:<your-user>`.

### Build the Images with NGINX Plus

> Note: You will need a valid NGINX Plus license certificate and key named `nginx-repo.crt` and `nginx-repo.key` in the
> root of this repo to build the NGINX Plus image.
> You will also need a valid NGINX Plus JSON Web Token (JWT) to deploy NGF with NGINX Plus. That JWT should be stored in the `license.jwt` file in the root of the `nginx-gateway-fabric/` directory. See the [documentation](https://docs.nginx.com/nginx-gateway-fabric/install/nginx-plus/) for instructions on how to download and set up the JWT.
> Additionally, you need to set the NGINX Plus usage endpoint in your environment. For development and testing, export `PLUS_USAGE_ENDPOINT=<N1 staging endpoint>`.

To build the NGINX Gateway Fabric and NGINX Plus container images from source run the following make command:

```makefile
make TAG=$(whoami) build-images-with-plus
```

This will build the docker images `nginx-gateway-fabric:<your-user>` and `nginx-gateway-fabric/nginx-plus:<your-user>`.

## Deploy on Kind

1. Create a `kind` cluster:

   To create a `kind` cluster with dual (IPv4 and IPv6) enabled:

   ```makefile
   make create-kind-cluster
   ```

   To create a `kind` cluster with IPv6 or IPv4 only, edit kind cluster config located at `nginx-gateway-fabric/config/cluster/kind-cluster.yaml`:

   ```yaml
   kind: Cluster
   apiVersion: kind.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
   nodes:
   - role: control-plane
   networking:
     ipFamily: ipv6
     apiServerAddress: 127.0.0.1
   ```

2. Load the previously built images onto your `kind` cluster:

   ```shell
   kind load docker-image nginx-gateway-fabric:$(whoami) nginx-gateway-fabric/nginx:$(whoami)
   ```

   or

   ```shell
   kind load docker-image nginx-gateway-fabric:$(whoami) nginx-gateway-fabric/nginx-plus:$(whoami)
   ```

3. Install Gateway API CRDs:

   ```shell
   kubectl kustomize config/crd/gateway-api/standard | kubectl apply -f -
   ```

   If you're implementing experimental Gateway API features, install Gateway API CRDs from the experimental channel:

   ```shell
   kubectl kustomize config/crd/gateway-api/experimental | kubectl apply -f -
   ```

4. Install NGF using your custom image and expose NGF with a NodePort Service:

   - To install with Helm (where your release name is `my-release`):

     ```shell
     helm install my-release ./charts/nginx-gateway-fabric --create-namespace --wait --set nginx.service.type=NodePort --set nginxGateway.image.repository=nginx-gateway-fabric --set nginxGateway.image.tag=$(whoami) --set nginxGateway.image.pullPolicy=Never --set nginx.image.repository=nginx-gateway-fabric/nginx --set nginx.image.tag=$(whoami) --set nginx.image.pullPolicy=Never -n nginx-gateway
     ```

   - To install NGINX Plus with Helm (where your release name is `my-release`):

     ```shell
     helm install my-release ./charts/nginx-gateway-fabric --create-namespace --wait --set nginx.service.type=NodePort --set nginxGateway.image.repository=nginx-gateway-fabric --set nginxGateway.image.tag=$(whoami) --set nginxGateway.image.pullPolicy=Never --set nginx.image.repository=nginx-gateway-fabric/nginx-plus --set nginx.image.tag=$(whoami) --set nginx.image.pullPolicy=Never --set nginx.plus=true -n nginx-gateway
     ```

   > For more information on Helm configuration options see the Helm [README](../../charts/nginx-gateway-fabric/README.md).

   - To install with manifests:

     The mainifests files are genarated using Helm from the [examples](/examples/helm) directory. To generate a custom one you can modify the `values.yaml` file in the example you want to use with the desired values and follow the instructions about [manifests generation](/examples/helm/README.md#manifests-generation).

     If the only change is the image repository and tag, you can update the `kustomization.yaml` file in `deploy/` with the desired values and deployment mainifest and run the following commands:

     ```shell
      kubectl apply --server-side -f deploy/crds.yaml
      kubectl kustomize deploy | kubectl apply -f -
     ```

  > For more information on how to use the manifests, see the [deployment manifests](/deploy/README.md) documentation.

### Run Examples

To make sure NGF is running properly, try out the [examples](/examples).

## Run the Unit Tests

To run all the unit tests, run the make unit-test command from the project's root directory:

```makefile
make unit-test
```

For more details on testing, see the [testing](/docs/developer/testing.md) documentation.

## Gateway API Conformance Testing

To run Gateway API conformance tests, please follow the instructions on [this](/tests/README.md#conformance-testing) page.

## Run the Linter

To lint the code, run the following make command from the project's root directory:

```makefile
make lint
```

> **Note**
> fieldalignment errors can be fixed by running: `fieldalignment -fix <path-to-package>`

## Run the Helm Linter

Run the following make command from the project's root directory to lint the Helm Chart code:

```shell
make lint-helm
```

## Update all the generated files

To update all the generated files, run the following make command from the project's root directory:

```makefile
make generate-all
```

input.rationale: nginx-gateway-fabric 是一个基于 controller-runtime 的 Kubernetes Gateway API 实现，控制面（Go）将 Gateway/Route 等资源翻译成 NGINX 配置，再通过 NGINX Agent（gRPC）下发到数据面。代码体量大、子系统多（CLI、Manager、自研 framework、状态/图、配置生成、Agent 通信、策略 CRD、WAF、可观测性、Inference 扩展、Plus 能力），因此采用「由浅入深、按数据流向分层」的学习顺序：先建立项目认知与运行能力（入门层），再沿真实调用链「CLI → Manager 组装 → 自研框架/事件管线 → 状态捕获与图构建 → 数据面配置 → NGINX 配置生成与模板 → Agent 下发 → 状态与策略」逐层深入（进阶层），最后聚焦扩展点、安全、可观测性、Plus、WAF、AI 推理扩展以及测试与二次开发（专家层）。每个单元解决一类问题，讲义之间用 depends_on 串成清晰依赖图，确保读者可从零开始、按真实源码顺序循序渐进。

units.title: 入门：项目认知、构建与部署上手

lectures.title: 项目概览：NGINX Gateway Fabric 是什么
lectures.topic: 介绍 NGINX Gateway Fabric 的定位：它是 Kubernetes Gateway API 的一个实现，以 NGINX 作为数据面，并使用 NGINX Agent 下发配置。
learning_goals: 理解 Gateway API 的核心资源（Gateway/GatewayClass/HTTPRoute 等）与 NGF 的角色
区分控制面与数据面，理解 NGINX Agent 的作用
了解 NGF 支持的 Gateway API、Kubernetes、NGINX/Plus 版本矩阵
minimal_modules: Gateway API 与 NGF 定位
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 README 并用自己的话写一段：NGF 相比一个普通 Ingress Controller 多了什么？为什么需要 GatewayClass。

lectures.title: 目录结构与代码组织
lectures.topic: 讲解仓库顶层目录与核心代码树的划分，重点是 cmd、internal/controller、internal/framework、apis。
learning_goals: 建立仓库整体地图，知道每个顶层目录负责什么
区分 internal/controller（产品逻辑）与 internal/framework（可复用框架）
找到 apis（自定义 CRD 类型）、deploy/charts（部署）、examples（示例）的位置
minimal_modules: controller 与 framework 的边界
lectures.practice_task: 画出一张仓库目录树，用三种颜色分别标注：入口、控制面产品逻辑、可复用框架。

lectures.title: 构建与本地运行方式
lectures.topic: 通过 Makefile 和开发 quickstart 介绍如何编译二进制、构建镜像、在 kind 集群中运行与调试 NGF。
learning_goals: 掌握 make build / build-images 等核心目标
了解 kind 集群创建、镜像加载、CRD 安装流程
知道如何运行单元测试与 lint
lectures.practice_task: 运行 make build 产出二进制，并执行 gateway --help 查看可用子命令。

lectures.title: 安装与部署：Helm 与 Manifests
lectures.topic: 介绍通过 Helm chart 与 deploy/ 目录的 manifests 安装 NGF 的方式，以及多种部署变体（default/plus/snippets/nodeport/openshift 等）。
learning_goals: 理解 Helm chart 结构与 values 的作用
区分 deploy/ 下的不同部署变体
知道 CRD 与控制面 Deployment 的安装顺序
minimal_modules: CRD 与 Deployment 安装顺序
lectures.practice_task: 用 helm install 在 kind 中部署 NGF，并通过 kubectl 查看 GatewayClass 是否就绪。

lectures.title: 运行第一个例子：路由一个应用
lectures.topic: 以 cafe-example 等示例为抓手，端到端体验：创建 Gateway/HTTPRoute，让 NGF 把流量路由到后端服务。
learning_goals: 用一个完整示例串联 Gateway/HTTPRoute/Service
观察 NGF 如何把路由翻译成 NGINX 行为
学会用 examples/ 目录查找用法
lectures.practice_task: 部署 examples 中的一个路由示例，用 curl 验证请求被正确转发到后端。

units.title: CLI 入口与控制面启动配置

lectures.title: 命令行子命令总览
lectures.topic: 解析 cmd/gateway 的 main 与 commands.go，理清 controller/generate-certs/initialize/sleep/endpoint-picker 五个子命令的职责。
learning_goals: 理解 cobra 命令的组装方式
区分控制面主命令与辅助命令（证书、初始化、endpoint-picker）
掌握命令行入口到业务逻辑的跳转点
lectures.practice_task: 在源码中标注每个子命令对应的处理函数入口，并说明哪个命令运行在控制面容器、哪个在 init 容器。

lectures.title: controller 命令与运行时配置
lectures.topic: 深入 controller 子命令的启动流程与 config.Config 结构，理解所有运行参数如何汇聚成控制面配置。
learning_goals: 掌握 controller 命令的关键 flag（gateway-ctlr-name、gatewayclass、nginx-plus 等）
理解 internal/controller/config/config.go 中 Config 等结构的作用
看清 flag 到 Config 再到 StartManager 的数据流
lectures.practice_task: 挑选 3 个 flag，追踪它们最终被哪个结构字段消费、影响哪个子系统。

lectures.title: 初始化命令与证书生成
lectures.topic: 讲解 initialize（init 容器写初始文件）与 generate-certs（自签 CA/服务端/客户端证书）两个命令。
learning_goals: 理解 init 容器为何需要 initialize 命令
掌握控制面↔数据面 mTLS 所需证书（server-tls/agent-tls）的生成
了解 NGINX Plus 场景下 deployment context 的生成
minimal_modules: 证书生成与 Secret 创建
lectures.practice_task: 梳理一张证书用途表：CA、server、client 各自服务端是谁、客户端是谁。

lectures.title: 参数校验与准入式校验机制
lectures.topic: 讲解 validation.go / validating_types.go 中对 flag 的校验机制，以及可复用的校验类型设计。
learning_goals: 掌握 controller name、资源名、端口、URL 等校验规则
理解 stringValidatingValue 等 pflag Value 类型的设计
了解 ensureNoPortCollisions 等一致性校验
lectures.practice_task: 为一个新的字符串 flag 添加一条校验规则，参考现有 validating value 实现。

units.title: Manager 组装与控制器注册

lectures.title: StartManager 全景：控制面如何被装配
lectures.topic: 以 manager.go 的 StartManager 为主线，看清控制面各子系统（处理器、生成器、Agent 服务、provisioner、telemetry、健康检查）如何被组装。
learning_goals: 掌握 StartManager 的整体装配顺序
理解 metrics、agent 服务、provisioner、telemetry、policy manager 的创建时机
看清依赖注入的组装方式
lectures.practice_task: 画出 StartManager 中各组件的创建与装配顺序图。

lectures.title: controller-runtime Manager 与缓存
lectures.topic: 讲解 createManager 与 buildManagerCache：如何创建 controller-runtime manager、配置 cache（含 transform/namespace 过滤）。
learning_goals: 理解 controller-runtime manager 的创建与选项
掌握 cache 的 transform 与 watch namespace 配置
了解 metrics/health server 的挂载
lectures.practice_task: 解释 cache transform 如何在不改业务逻辑的情况下降低内存占用。

lectures.title: 控制器注册与 CRD 存在性发现
lectures.topic: 讲解 registerControllers 如何注册各类资源控制器，以及 crd/discovery 如何按 CRD 是否存在来动态启用/禁用控制器。
learning_goals: 掌握控制器注册表 ctlrCfg 的结构
理解按 CRD 存在性过滤控制器的机制
了解 feature flag 与控制器的关系
lectures.practice_task: 追踪一个 Gateway API 资源类型的控制器是如何被注册并被 field index 配置的。

lectures.title: Leader Election 与 Runnables
lectures.topic: 讲解框架中 Leader/LeaderOrNonLeader runnable 包装与 leader election，以及 CronJob 周期任务机制。
learning_goals: 理解哪些工作只在 leader 上运行
掌握 framework/runnables 的 Leader 包装语义
了解 CronJob 周期任务（如 telemetry）的实现
lectures.practice_task: 判断 telemetry/状态更新等任务应使用 Leader 还是 LeaderOrNonLeader，并说明理由。

units.title: 事件管线：从控制器到处理器

lectures.title: 自研框架：控制器抽象与过滤机制
lectures.topic: 讲解 internal/framework/controller 的 Register/Reconciler 抽象，以及 predicate/filter/index/cache 多层过滤。
learning_goals: 理解 Reconciler 如何把资源变更转成 Upsert/Delete 事件
掌握 predicate、namespacedName filter、field index、cache transform 多层过滤
了解 WithOnlyMetadata 等优化选项
lectures.practice_task: 为某类资源添加一个 predicate，只在某注解变化时触发事件。

lectures.title: 事件循环与批处理（双缓冲）
lectures.topic: 讲解 internal/framework/events 的 EventLoop：如何用双缓冲批处理事件以减少 NGINX reload 次数，以及首批事件如何由 FirstEventBatchPreparer 准备。
learning_goals: 理解事件批处理与双缓冲机制
掌握首批事件（集群初始状态）的准备方式
了解 EventHandler/EventBatch 的抽象
lectures.practice_task: 解释为什么双缓冲能把多次资源变更合并成一次 NGINX 配置更新。

lectures.title: EventHandler：事件批次的总编排
lectures.topic: 讲解 internal/controller/handler.go 的 eventHandlerImpl：它如何编排 ChangeProcessor、Generator、NginxUpdater、StatusUpdater 完成一轮处理。
learning_goals: 掌握 HandleEventBatch 的主流程
理解捕获变更→构建图→生成配置→下发→更新状态的编排顺序
了解健康检查就绪与 statusQueue 的协作
lectures.practice_task: 画出一次事件批次在 handler 中的完整调用时序图。

lectures.title: ChangeProcessor 与状态存储
lectures.topic: 讲解 state.ChangeProcessor 如何捕获资源增删改、维护 ClusterState/对象存储，并在 Process 时输出图。
learning_goals: 理解 CaptureUpsertChange/CaptureDeleteChange 的捕获机制
掌握对象存储 store 与 changed predicate
了解 Process 如何把变更转化为 graph
lectures.practice_task: 说明 changed_predicate 如何避免无意义的状态重建。

units.title: 图构建与数据面配置

lectures.title: Graph：把 Gateway API 资源变成内部模型
lectures.topic: 讲解 state/graph 如何把原始 Gateway/GatewayClass 等资源构建成内部 Graph，包括校验与条件收集。
learning_goals: 理解 Graph 数据结构与构建入口
掌握 GatewayClass/Gateway 到图的映射
了解图构建中的校验与 conditions 收集
lectures.practice_task: 追踪一个 GatewayClass 被接受/拒绝的过程在图中如何体现。

lectures.title: 路由与监听器：HTTP/gRPC/TCP/TLS/UDP
lectures.topic: 讲解各类 Route 与 Listener 在图中的处理：HTTPRoute、GRPCRoute、TCPRoute、TLSRoute、UDPRoute 以及 ListenerSet。
learning_goals: 掌握不同 Route 类型在图中的统一处理（route_common）
理解 Listener 端口/协议/主机名匹配
了解 ListenerSet 扩展监听器的能力
lectures.practice_task: 为一个 HTTPRoute 追踪它在图中被绑定到哪个 Listener、生成哪些 backendRefs。

lectures.title: 后端解析：Service、BackendRef、ReferenceGrant
lectures.topic: 讲解后端如何被解析：BackendRef → Service → Endpoints（resolver），以及跨命名空间引用的 ReferenceGrant 校验。
learning_goals: 理解 BackendRef 到 Service/Endpoints 的解析
掌握 ServiceResolver 的工作方式
了解 ReferenceGrant 跨命名空间授权
lectures.practice_task: 构造一个跨命名空间后端引用，验证没有 ReferenceGrant 时图如何标记错误条件。

lectures.title: dataplane.Configuration：图的最终配置表示
lectures.topic: 讲解 BuildConfiguration 如何把 Graph 转成 dataplane.Configuration（Servers/Upstreams/SSL/证书等），这是交给配置生成器的输入。
learning_goals: 理解 dataplane.Configuration 的结构与构建
掌握 SSL 证书对、L4/Stream 服务的构建
了解配置在交给生成器前的最终形态
lectures.practice_task: 解释 Graph 与 dataplane.Configuration 的区别：为什么需要这层中间表示。

units.title: NGINX 配置生成与模板

lectures.title: 配置生成器总览：从 Configuration 到 []File
lectures.topic: 讲解 nginx/config/generator.go 的 Generator 接口与实现：如何把 dataplane.Configuration 渲染成一组 NGINX 配置文件。
learning_goals: 掌握 Generator.Generate 的整体流程
理解 executeConfigTemplates 的多步模板执行链
了解生成文件如何组织成 conf.d/stream-conf.d 等目录
lectures.practice_task: 在 Generate 中找到生成 servers、upstreams、maps 各自对应的执行函数。

lectures.title: nginx.conf 骨架与模板体系
lectures.topic: 讲解静态 nginx.conf 骨架与各类模板（main/events/base http）如何配合，以及 Go text/template 的组织方式。
learning_goals: 理解静态 nginx.conf 与生成片段的 include 关系
掌握 main/base http/events 模板的职责
了解模板常量与执行函数的对应
lectures.practice_task: 对照 nginx.conf 找出每个 include 分别由哪个模板执行函数生成。

lectures.title: Servers、Upstreams 与 Locations 生成
lectures.topic: 深入 HTTP server/upstream/location 块的生成，包括负载均衡、keepalive、zone 大小等细节。
learning_goals: 掌握 servers.go/upstreams.go 的生成逻辑
理解 upstream zone 大小、keepalive 等 NGINX 指令
了解 location 路由匹配的生成
lectures.practice_task: 修改一处后端配置，观察生成的 upstream 块中 zone 与 keepalive 指令的变化。

lectures.title: Stream 配置：TCP/UDP/TLS Passthrough
lectures.topic: 讲解 stream 上下文的生成：TCPRoute/UDPRoute/TLSRoute（passthrough）对应的 stream server/upstream/map。
learning_goals: 理解 stream 上下文与 http 上下文的区别
掌握 L4 虚拟服务器与 stream upstream 的生成
了解 TLS passthrough（SNI）的处理
lectures.practice_task: 为一个 TCPRoute 追踪它最终生成的 stream server 与 upstream 配置。

units.title: 数据面通信：通过 NGINX Agent 下发配置

lectures.title: NginxUpdater 与 gRPC Agent 通信
lectures.topic: 讲解 NginxUpdater 接口与 agent.go，以及控制面侧 gRPC 服务（grpc/、connections、messenger）如何与数据面 Agent 通信。
learning_goals: 理解 NginxUpdater.UpdateConfig 的职责
掌握控制面 gRPC 服务与连接管理
了解 messenger 广播与 filewatcher 机制
lectures.practice_task: 梳理一次配置更新从 UpdateConfig 到 Agent 收到的完整 gRPC 调用链。

lectures.title: 配置文件下发与 Deployment 管理
lectures.topic: 讲解配置文件如何按 Deployment 维度管理并推送到各数据面 Pod，以及命令（reload）服务。
learning_goals: 理解 DeploymentStore 如何按数据面 Deployment 组织文件
掌握文件传输（GetFile）流程
了解 reload/command 服务
lectures.practice_task: 解释当存在多个数据面 Pod 时，配置如何保证一致地应用到每个 Pod。

units.title: 状态、条件与策略 CRD

lectures.title: Conditions 体系与状态更新
lectures.topic: 讲解条件（Conditions）的定义与含义，以及 status updater/queue 如何异步地把状态写回 Kubernetes 资源。
learning_goals: 掌握 Conditions 的标准集合与含义
理解 status.Queue 与 GroupUpdater 的异步更新
了解 leader-aware 的状态写入
lectures.practice_task: 给定一个错误场景，判断应该给 Gateway/HTTPRoute 设置哪些 Conditions。

lectures.title: 自定义 CRD 与策略附着（Policy Attachment）
lectures.topic: 讲解 apis 下的 NGF 自定义资源（NginxProxy、ClientSettingsPolicy 等）及其 Gateway API 策略附着模型，以及图中的策略解析。
learning_goals: 掌握 v1alpha1/v1alpha2 中的 CRD 类型与用途
理解 Direct/Inherited 策略附着模型
了解 graph/policies.go 如何解析与附着策略
lectures.practice_task: 为一个 HTTPRoute 附着一个 ClientSettingsPolicy，并追踪它在图中如何被解析。

lectures.title: 策略到 NGINX 指令的生成
lectures.topic: 讲解 nginx/config/policies 下的策略生成器（复合生成器、各类 policy 生成器）如何把策略翻译成 NGINX 指令。
learning_goals: 掌握复合策略生成器的设计
理解 clientsettings/proxysettings/ratelimit 等如何生成指令
了解策略校验 validator 的作用
lectures.practice_task: 实现或扩展一个策略生成器，把一个策略字段翻译成对应的 NGINX 指令。

units.title: Provisioner：按 Gateway 配置数据面

lectures.title: Provisioner：为每个 Gateway 创建/回收 NGINX 资源
lectures.topic: 讲解 provisioner 如何根据 Gateway 资源动态创建/删除数据面 NGINX Deployment 等资源，及其事件循环与状态存储。
learning_goals: 理解 Provisioner 接口与 NginxProvisioner 的职责
掌握 provisioner eventloop 与 store
了解数据面资源生命周期与 Gateway 的绑定关系
lectures.practice_task: 说明创建一个新 Gateway 时，provisioner 会触发哪些数据面资源的创建。

units.title: WAF（F5 App Protect）集成

lectures.title: WAFPolicy 与 Bundle 模型
lectures.topic: 讲解 WAFPolicy CRD 的定义、WAF bundle 的来源（HTTP/NIM/N1C/PLM）以及 waf/v1 中 PLM 状态类型。
learning_goals: 掌握 WAFPolicy 的字段与 bundle 来源
理解 PLM 管理的 APPolicy/APLogConf 状态
了解 bundle 校验（checksum）与失败行为
lectures.practice_task: 为 WAFPolicy 设计一个 bundle 来源选择，并说明每种来源的鉴权方式差异。

lectures.title: Bundle 拉取、轮询与下发
lectures.topic: 讲解 framework/waf 的 fetch（含 S3）与 poller：如何拉取、校验、周期轮询 bundle 并推送到数据面。
learning_goals: 掌握 Fetcher 接口与 HTTP/S3 拉取
理解 poller Manager 的生命周期与轮询
了解 bundle 变更如何触发重新配置
lectures.practice_task: 梳理 bundle checksum 变化时，从 poller 到数据面 Pod 的完整通知链路。

units.title: 可观测性：指标、遥测与链路追踪

lectures.title: Prometheus 指标
lectures.topic: 讲解 controller/metrics 的指标采集与暴露，包括事件批次处理耗时等控制器指标。
learning_goals: 掌握指标的注册与暴露方式
理解 ControllerCollector 采集的内容
了解 NginxProxy 中 metrics 端口配置
lectures.practice_task: 找到事件批次处理耗时指标的定义，并用 PromQL 写一条告警思路。

lectures.title: 产品遥测（Telemetry）
lectures.topic: 讲解 controller/telemetry 的数据采集、Avro schema（data.avdl）与导出，以及周期上报机制。
learning_goals: 理解 DataCollector 采集哪些集群/产品信息
掌握 data.avdl schema 与 Data 结构
了解 telemetry 周期上报与可关闭开关
lectures.practice_task: 列出 telemetry 上报的字段，并说明哪些可以通过 flag 关闭。

lectures.title: OpenTelemetry 链路追踪
lectures.topic: 讲解 ObservabilityPolicy 与 telemetry 模板如何生成 OpenTelemetry 配置，实现 HTTP/gRPC 路由的分布式追踪。
learning_goals: 掌握 ObservabilityPolicy 的 tracing 配置
理解 otel 模板生成与采样策略
了解 span 属性与上下文传播
lectures.practice_task: 为一个 HTTPRoute 启用追踪，并追踪 tracing 配置如何进入生成的 nginx 配置。

units.title: 高级特性：推理扩展、NGINX Plus、TLS 与 Snippets/认证

lectures.title: Gateway API 推理扩展（Inference）
lectures.topic: 讲解 endpoint-picker 子命令与 InferencePool 在图中的处理，理解 NGF 如何与 Gateway API Inference Extension 协作。
learning_goals: 掌握 endpoint-picker shim 服务器的作用
理解 InferencePool 在 graph 中的处理
了解 NGINX 与外部 EPP 的交互（ext proc 头）
lectures.practice_task: 说明 endpoint-picker 如何把 NGINX 的请求转发给真正的 EndpointPicker 服务。

lectures.title: NGINX Plus 能力：动态上游、授权与用量上报
lectures.topic: 讲解 NGINX Plus 相对 OSS 的差异：动态 upstream 更新、Plus API/mgmt 配置、licensing 与 usage report。
learning_goals: 掌握 UpdateUpstreamServers 动态更新机制
理解 Plus mgmt/usage report 配置生成
了解 licensing deployment context 的收集
lectures.practice_task: 对比 OSS 与 Plus 在 upstream 更新路径上的差异，说明 Plus 为何能不 reload。

lectures.title: TLS 安全：前端证书、后端 TLS 与 Agent mTLS
lectures.topic: 讲解前端 TLS 终止、BackendTLSPolicy（后端 mTLS/CA）、以及控制面↔数据面 Agent 的 mTLS 安全。
learning_goals: 掌握前端 TLS 证书对的处理
理解 BackendTLSPolicy 在图中的解析
了解 Agent mTLS 证书的生成与使用
lectures.practice_task: 为一个 HTTPS 后端配置 BackendTLSPolicy，并追踪 CA bundle 如何进入 NGINX 配置。

lectures.title: SnippetsFilter 与认证过滤器
lectures.topic: 讲解 SnippetsFilter/SnippetsPolicy（注入 NGINX 配置片段）与 AuthenticationFilter（Basic/JWT/OIDC）两种扩展机制。
learning_goals: 掌握 snippets 的允许上下文与注入方式
理解认证过滤器的多种认证类型
了解这些扩展的安全约束与 Plus 依赖
lectures.practice_task: 为一个 HTTPRoute 通过 SnippetsFilter 注入一条自定义 NGINX 指令，并验证生效。

units.title: 测试、性能与二次开发

lectures.title: 测试体系：单元、Fake 与集成测试
lectures.topic: 讲解 NGF 的测试方法：counterfeiter 生成的 fake、envtest 集成测试、conformance 测试的组织。
learning_goals: 掌握 controllerfakes/statefakes 等 fake 的生成与使用
理解 controller_suite_test 的 envtest 集成测试
了解 Gateway API conformance 测试入口
lectures.practice_task: 为一个新函数编写单元测试，并使用现有 fake 替换依赖。

lectures.title: 并发、性能与可靠性设计
lectures.topic: 从架构视角讲解 NGF 的并发与可靠性：事件双缓冲、leader 选举、冲突排序（ngfsort）、健康检查与就绪。
learning_goals: 理解事件双缓冲对吞吐与 reload 次数的影响
掌握 leader 选举对一致性的保障
了解 ngfsort 在冲突解析中的作用
lectures.practice_task: 分析一次资源高频变更场景下，双缓冲如何降低 NGINX reload 次数。

lectures.title: 二次开发：新增功能与扩展点
lectures.topic: 结合开发文档，讲解如何为 NGF 新增一个功能（如新策略/新 CRD），贯穿 API→图→配置生成→测试的完整改动。
learning_goals: 掌握新增 CRD/策略的完整改动链路
理解 implementing-a-feature 的开发规范
了解代码生成（generate-all）与 PR 流程
lectures.practice_task: 设计一个新的简单策略 CRD，列出从 API 定义到配置生成需要改动的文件清单。

data.head: 5504d0d7de1706d1bda281281fbb0753a7b32806
data.rationale: nginx-gateway-fabric 是一个基于 controller-runtime 的 Kubernetes Gateway API 实现，控制面（Go）将 Gateway/Route 等资源翻译成 NGINX 配置，再通过 NGINX Agent（gRPC）下发到数据面。代码体量大、子系统多（CLI、Manager、自研 framework、状态/图、配置生成、Agent 通信、策略 CRD、WAF、可观测性、Inference 扩展、Plus 能力），因此采用「由浅入深、按数据流向分层」的学习顺序：先建立项目认知与运行能力（入门层），再沿真实调用链「CLI → Manager 组装 → 自研框架/事件管线 → 状态捕获与图构建 → 数据面配置 → NGINX 配置生成与模板 → Agent 下发 → 状态与策略」逐层深入（进阶层），最后聚焦扩展点、安全、可观测性、Plus、WAF、AI 推理扩展以及测试与二次开发（专家层）。每个单元解决一类问题，讲义之间用 depends_on 串成清晰依赖图，确保读者可从零开始、按真实源码顺序循序渐进。

units.title: 入门：项目认知、构建与部署上手

lectures.title: 项目概览：NGINX Gateway Fabric 是什么
lectures.topic: 介绍 NGINX Gateway Fabric 的定位：它是 Kubernetes Gateway API 的一个实现，以 NGINX 作为数据面，并使用 NGINX Agent 下发配置。
learning_goals: 理解 Gateway API 的核心资源（Gateway/GatewayClass/HTTPRoute 等）与 NGF 的角色
区分控制面与数据面，理解 NGINX Agent 的作用
了解 NGF 支持的 Gateway API、Kubernetes、NGINX/Plus 版本矩阵
minimal_modules: Gateway API 与 NGF 定位
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 README 并用自己的话写一段：NGF 相比一个普通 Ingress Controller 多了什么？为什么需要 GatewayClass。

lectures.title: 目录结构与代码组织
lectures.topic: 讲解仓库顶层目录与核心代码树的划分，重点是 cmd、internal/controller、internal/framework、apis。
learning_goals: 建立仓库整体地图，知道每个顶层目录负责什么
区分 internal/controller（产品逻辑）与 internal/framework（可复用框架）
找到 apis（自定义 CRD 类型）、deploy/charts（部署）、examples（示例）的位置
minimal_modules: controller 与 framework 的边界
lectures.practice_task: 画出一张仓库目录树，用三种颜色分别标注：入口、控制面产品逻辑、可复用框架。

lectures.title: 构建与本地运行方式
lectures.topic: 通过 Makefile 和开发 quickstart 介绍如何编译二进制、构建镜像、在 kind 集群中运行与调试 NGF。
learning_goals: 掌握 make build / build-images 等核心目标
了解 kind 集群创建、镜像加载、CRD 安装流程
知道如何运行单元测试与 lint
lectures.practice_task: 运行 make build 产出二进制，并执行 gateway --help 查看可用子命令。

lectures.title: 安装与部署：Helm 与 Manifests
lectures.topic: 介绍通过 Helm chart 与 deploy/ 目录的 manifests 安装 NGF 的方式，以及多种部署变体（default/plus/snippets/nodeport/openshift 等）。
learning_goals: 理解 Helm chart 结构与 values 的作用
区分 deploy/ 下的不同部署变体
知道 CRD 与控制面 Deployment 的安装顺序
minimal_modules: CRD 与 Deployment 安装顺序
lectures.practice_task: 用 helm install 在 kind 中部署 NGF，并通过 kubectl 查看 GatewayClass 是否就绪。

lectures.title: 运行第一个例子：路由一个应用
lectures.topic: 以 cafe-example 等示例为抓手，端到端体验：创建 Gateway/HTTPRoute，让 NGF 把流量路由到后端服务。
learning_goals: 用一个完整示例串联 Gateway/HTTPRoute/Service
观察 NGF 如何把路由翻译成 NGINX 行为
学会用 examples/ 目录查找用法
lectures.practice_task: 部署 examples 中的一个路由示例，用 curl 验证请求被正确转发到后端。

units.title: CLI 入口与控制面启动配置

lectures.title: 命令行子命令总览
lectures.topic: 解析 cmd/gateway 的 main 与 commands.go，理清 controller/generate-certs/initialize/sleep/endpoint-picker 五个子命令的职责。
learning_goals: 理解 cobra 命令的组装方式
区分控制面主命令与辅助命令（证书、初始化、endpoint-picker）
掌握命令行入口到业务逻辑的跳转点
lectures.practice_task: 在源码中标注每个子命令对应的处理函数入口，并说明哪个命令运行在控制面容器、哪个在 init 容器。

lectures.title: controller 命令与运行时配置
lectures.topic: 深入 controller 子命令的启动流程与 config.Config 结构，理解所有运行参数如何汇聚成控制面配置。
learning_goals: 掌握 controller 命令的关键 flag（gateway-ctlr-name、gatewayclass、nginx-plus 等）
理解 internal/controller/config/config.go 中 Config 等结构的作用
看清 flag 到 Config 再到 StartManager 的数据流
lectures.practice_task: 挑选 3 个 flag，追踪它们最终被哪个结构字段消费、影响哪个子系统。

lectures.title: 初始化命令与证书生成
lectures.topic: 讲解 initialize（init 容器写初始文件）与 generate-certs（自签 CA/服务端/客户端证书）两个命令。
learning_goals: 理解 init 容器为何需要 initialize 命令
掌握控制面↔数据面 mTLS 所需证书（server-tls/agent-tls）的生成
了解 NGINX Plus 场景下 deployment context 的生成
minimal_modules: 证书生成与 Secret 创建
lectures.practice_task: 梳理一张证书用途表：CA、server、client 各自服务端是谁、客户端是谁。

lectures.title: 参数校验与准入式校验机制
lectures.topic: 讲解 validation.go / validating_types.go 中对 flag 的校验机制，以及可复用的校验类型设计。
learning_goals: 掌握 controller name、资源名、端口、URL 等校验规则
理解 stringValidatingValue 等 pflag Value 类型的设计
了解 ensureNoPortCollisions 等一致性校验
lectures.practice_task: 为一个新的字符串 flag 添加一条校验规则，参考现有 validating value 实现。

units.title: Manager 组装与控制器注册

lectures.title: StartManager 全景：控制面如何被装配
lectures.topic: 以 manager.go 的 StartManager 为主线，看清控制面各子系统（处理器、生成器、Agent 服务、provisioner、telemetry、健康检查）如何被组装。
learning_goals: 掌握 StartManager 的整体装配顺序
理解 metrics、agent 服务、provisioner、telemetry、policy manager 的创建时机
看清依赖注入的组装方式
lectures.practice_task: 画出 StartManager 中各组件的创建与装配顺序图。

lectures.title: controller-runtime Manager 与缓存
lectures.topic: 讲解 createManager 与 buildManagerCache：如何创建 controller-runtime manager、配置 cache（含 transform/namespace 过滤）。
learning_goals: 理解 controller-runtime manager 的创建与选项
掌握 cache 的 transform 与 watch namespace 配置
了解 metrics/health server 的挂载
lectures.practice_task: 解释 cache transform 如何在不改业务逻辑的情况下降低内存占用。

lectures.title: 控制器注册与 CRD 存在性发现
lectures.topic: 讲解 registerControllers 如何注册各类资源控制器，以及 crd/discovery 如何按 CRD 是否存在来动态启用/禁用控制器。
learning_goals: 掌握控制器注册表 ctlrCfg 的结构
理解按 CRD 存在性过滤控制器的机制
了解 feature flag 与控制器的关系
lectures.practice_task: 追踪一个 Gateway API 资源类型的控制器是如何被注册并被 field index 配置的。

lectures.title: Leader Election 与 Runnables
lectures.topic: 讲解框架中 Leader/LeaderOrNonLeader runnable 包装与 leader election，以及 CronJob 周期任务机制。
learning_goals: 理解哪些工作只在 leader 上运行
掌握 framework/runnables 的 Leader 包装语义
了解 CronJob 周期任务（如 telemetry）的实现
lectures.practice_task: 判断 telemetry/状态更新等任务应使用 Leader 还是 LeaderOrNonLeader，并说明理由。

units.title: 事件管线：从控制器到处理器

lectures.title: 自研框架：控制器抽象与过滤机制
lectures.topic: 讲解 internal/framework/controller 的 Register/Reconciler 抽象，以及 predicate/filter/index/cache 多层过滤。
learning_goals: 理解 Reconciler 如何把资源变更转成 Upsert/Delete 事件
掌握 predicate、namespacedName filter、field index、cache transform 多层过滤
了解 WithOnlyMetadata 等优化选项
lectures.practice_task: 为某类资源添加一个 predicate，只在某注解变化时触发事件。

lectures.title: 事件循环与批处理（双缓冲）
lectures.topic: 讲解 internal/framework/events 的 EventLoop：如何用双缓冲批处理事件以减少 NGINX reload 次数，以及首批事件如何由 FirstEventBatchPreparer 准备。
learning_goals: 理解事件批处理与双缓冲机制
掌握首批事件（集群初始状态）的准备方式
了解 EventHandler/EventBatch 的抽象
lectures.practice_task: 解释为什么双缓冲能把多次资源变更合并成一次 NGINX 配置更新。

lectures.title: EventHandler：事件批次的总编排
lectures.topic: 讲解 internal/controller/handler.go 的 eventHandlerImpl：它如何编排 ChangeProcessor、Generator、NginxUpdater、StatusUpdater 完成一轮处理。
learning_goals: 掌握 HandleEventBatch 的主流程
理解捕获变更→构建图→生成配置→下发→更新状态的编排顺序
了解健康检查就绪与 statusQueue 的协作
lectures.practice_task: 画出一次事件批次在 handler 中的完整调用时序图。

lectures.title: ChangeProcessor 与状态存储
lectures.topic: 讲解 state.ChangeProcessor 如何捕获资源增删改、维护 ClusterState/对象存储，并在 Process 时输出图。
learning_goals: 理解 CaptureUpsertChange/CaptureDeleteChange 的捕获机制
掌握对象存储 store 与 changed predicate
了解 Process 如何把变更转化为 graph
lectures.practice_task: 说明 changed_predicate 如何避免无意义的状态重建。

units.title: 图构建与数据面配置

lectures.title: Graph：把 Gateway API 资源变成内部模型
lectures.topic: 讲解 state/graph 如何把原始 Gateway/GatewayClass 等资源构建成内部 Graph，包括校验与条件收集。
learning_goals: 理解 Graph 数据结构与构建入口
掌握 GatewayClass/Gateway 到图的映射
了解图构建中的校验与 conditions 收集
lectures.practice_task: 追踪一个 GatewayClass 被接受/拒绝的过程在图中如何体现。

lectures.title: 路由与监听器：HTTP/gRPC/TCP/TLS/UDP
lectures.topic: 讲解各类 Route 与 Listener 在图中的处理：HTTPRoute、GRPCRoute、TCPRoute、TLSRoute、UDPRoute 以及 ListenerSet。
learning_goals: 掌握不同 Route 类型在图中的统一处理（route_common）
理解 Listener 端口/协议/主机名匹配
了解 ListenerSet 扩展监听器的能力
lectures.practice_task: 为一个 HTTPRoute 追踪它在图中被绑定到哪个 Listener、生成哪些 backendRefs。

lectures.title: 后端解析：Service、BackendRef、ReferenceGrant
lectures.topic: 讲解后端如何被解析：BackendRef → Service → Endpoints（resolver），以及跨命名空间引用的 ReferenceGrant 校验。
learning_goals: 理解 BackendRef 到 Service/Endpoints 的解析
掌握 ServiceResolver 的工作方式
了解 ReferenceGrant 跨命名空间授权
lectures.practice_task: 构造一个跨命名空间后端引用，验证没有 ReferenceGrant 时图如何标记错误条件。

lectures.title: dataplane.Configuration：图的最终配置表示
lectures.topic: 讲解 BuildConfiguration 如何把 Graph 转成 dataplane.Configuration（Servers/Upstreams/SSL/证书等），这是交给配置生成器的输入。
learning_goals: 理解 dataplane.Configuration 的结构与构建
掌握 SSL 证书对、L4/Stream 服务的构建
了解配置在交给生成器前的最终形态
lectures.practice_task: 解释 Graph 与 dataplane.Configuration 的区别：为什么需要这层中间表示。

units.title: NGINX 配置生成与模板

lectures.title: 配置生成器总览：从 Configuration 到 []File
lectures.topic: 讲解 nginx/config/generator.go 的 Generator 接口与实现：如何把 dataplane.Configuration 渲染成一组 NGINX 配置文件。
learning_goals: 掌握 Generator.Generate 的整体流程
理解 executeConfigTemplates 的多步模板执行链
了解生成文件如何组织成 conf.d/stream-conf.d 等目录
lectures.practice_task: 在 Generate 中找到生成 servers、upstreams、maps 各自对应的执行函数。

lectures.title: nginx.conf 骨架与模板体系
lectures.topic: 讲解静态 nginx.conf 骨架与各类模板（main/events/base http）如何配合，以及 Go text/template 的组织方式。
learning_goals: 理解静态 nginx.conf 与生成片段的 include 关系
掌握 main/base http/events 模板的职责
了解模板常量与执行函数的对应
lectures.practice_task: 对照 nginx.conf 找出每个 include 分别由哪个模板执行函数生成。

lectures.title: Servers、Upstreams 与 Locations 生成
lectures.topic: 深入 HTTP server/upstream/location 块的生成，包括负载均衡、keepalive、zone 大小等细节。
learning_goals: 掌握 servers.go/upstreams.go 的生成逻辑
理解 upstream zone 大小、keepalive 等 NGINX 指令
了解 location 路由匹配的生成
lectures.practice_task: 修改一处后端配置，观察生成的 upstream 块中 zone 与 keepalive 指令的变化。

lectures.title: Stream 配置：TCP/UDP/TLS Passthrough
lectures.topic: 讲解 stream 上下文的生成：TCPRoute/UDPRoute/TLSRoute（passthrough）对应的 stream server/upstream/map。
learning_goals: 理解 stream 上下文与 http 上下文的区别
掌握 L4 虚拟服务器与 stream upstream 的生成
了解 TLS passthrough（SNI）的处理
lectures.practice_task: 为一个 TCPRoute 追踪它最终生成的 stream server 与 upstream 配置。

units.title: 数据面通信：通过 NGINX Agent 下发配置

lectures.title: NginxUpdater 与 gRPC Agent 通信
lectures.topic: 讲解 NginxUpdater 接口与 agent.go，以及控制面侧 gRPC 服务（grpc/、connections、messenger）如何与数据面 Agent 通信。
learning_goals: 理解 NginxUpdater.UpdateConfig 的职责
掌握控制面 gRPC 服务与连接管理
了解 messenger 广播与 filewatcher 机制
lectures.practice_task: 梳理一次配置更新从 UpdateConfig 到 Agent 收到的完整 gRPC 调用链。

lectures.title: 配置文件下发与 Deployment 管理
lectures.topic: 讲解配置文件如何按 Deployment 维度管理并推送到各数据面 Pod，以及命令（reload）服务。
learning_goals: 理解 DeploymentStore 如何按数据面 Deployment 组织文件
掌握文件传输（GetFile）流程
了解 reload/command 服务
lectures.practice_task: 解释当存在多个数据面 Pod 时，配置如何保证一致地应用到每个 Pod。

units.title: 状态、条件与策略 CRD

lectures.title: Conditions 体系与状态更新
lectures.topic: 讲解条件（Conditions）的定义与含义，以及 status updater/queue 如何异步地把状态写回 Kubernetes 资源。
learning_goals: 掌握 Conditions 的标准集合与含义
理解 status.Queue 与 GroupUpdater 的异步更新
了解 leader-aware 的状态写入
lectures.practice_task: 给定一个错误场景，判断应该给 Gateway/HTTPRoute 设置哪些 Conditions。

lectures.title: 自定义 CRD 与策略附着（Policy Attachment）
lectures.topic: 讲解 apis 下的 NGF 自定义资源（NginxProxy、ClientSettingsPolicy 等）及其 Gateway API 策略附着模型，以及图中的策略解析。
learning_goals: 掌握 v1alpha1/v1alpha2 中的 CRD 类型与用途
理解 Direct/Inherited 策略附着模型
了解 graph/policies.go 如何解析与附着策略
lectures.practice_task: 为一个 HTTPRoute 附着一个 ClientSettingsPolicy，并追踪它在图中如何被解析。

lectures.title: 策略到 NGINX 指令的生成
lectures.topic: 讲解 nginx/config/policies 下的策略生成器（复合生成器、各类 policy 生成器）如何把策略翻译成 NGINX 指令。
learning_goals: 掌握复合策略生成器的设计
理解 clientsettings/proxysettings/ratelimit 等如何生成指令
了解策略校验 validator 的作用
lectures.practice_task: 实现或扩展一个策略生成器，把一个策略字段翻译成对应的 NGINX 指令。

units.title: Provisioner：按 Gateway 配置数据面

lectures.title: Provisioner：为每个 Gateway 创建/回收 NGINX 资源
lectures.topic: 讲解 provisioner 如何根据 Gateway 资源动态创建/删除数据面 NGINX Deployment 等资源，及其事件循环与状态存储。
learning_goals: 理解 Provisioner 接口与 NginxProvisioner 的职责
掌握 provisioner eventloop 与 store
了解数据面资源生命周期与 Gateway 的绑定关系
lectures.practice_task: 说明创建一个新 Gateway 时，provisioner 会触发哪些数据面资源的创建。

units.title: WAF（F5 App Protect）集成

lectures.title: WAFPolicy 与 Bundle 模型
lectures.topic: 讲解 WAFPolicy CRD 的定义、WAF bundle 的来源（HTTP/NIM/N1C/PLM）以及 waf/v1 中 PLM 状态类型。
learning_goals: 掌握 WAFPolicy 的字段与 bundle 来源
理解 PLM 管理的 APPolicy/APLogConf 状态
了解 bundle 校验（checksum）与失败行为
lectures.practice_task: 为 WAFPolicy 设计一个 bundle 来源选择，并说明每种来源的鉴权方式差异。

lectures.title: Bundle 拉取、轮询与下发
lectures.topic: 讲解 framework/waf 的 fetch（含 S3）与 poller：如何拉取、校验、周期轮询 bundle 并推送到数据面。
learning_goals: 掌握 Fetcher 接口与 HTTP/S3 拉取
理解 poller Manager 的生命周期与轮询
了解 bundle 变更如何触发重新配置
lectures.practice_task: 梳理 bundle checksum 变化时，从 poller 到数据面 Pod 的完整通知链路。

units.title: 可观测性：指标、遥测与链路追踪

lectures.title: Prometheus 指标
lectures.topic: 讲解 controller/metrics 的指标采集与暴露，包括事件批次处理耗时等控制器指标。
learning_goals: 掌握指标的注册与暴露方式
理解 ControllerCollector 采集的内容
了解 NginxProxy 中 metrics 端口配置
lectures.practice_task: 找到事件批次处理耗时指标的定义，并用 PromQL 写一条告警思路。

lectures.title: 产品遥测（Telemetry）
lectures.topic: 讲解 controller/telemetry 的数据采集、Avro schema（data.avdl）与导出，以及周期上报机制。
learning_goals: 理解 DataCollector 采集哪些集群/产品信息
掌握 data.avdl schema 与 Data 结构
了解 telemetry 周期上报与可关闭开关
lectures.practice_task: 列出 telemetry 上报的字段，并说明哪些可以通过 flag 关闭。

lectures.title: OpenTelemetry 链路追踪
lectures.topic: 讲解 ObservabilityPolicy 与 telemetry 模板如何生成 OpenTelemetry 配置，实现 HTTP/gRPC 路由的分布式追踪。
learning_goals: 掌握 ObservabilityPolicy 的 tracing 配置
理解 otel 模板生成与采样策略
了解 span 属性与上下文传播
lectures.practice_task: 为一个 HTTPRoute 启用追踪，并追踪 tracing 配置如何进入生成的 nginx 配置。

units.title: 高级特性：推理扩展、NGINX Plus、TLS 与 Snippets/认证

lectures.title: Gateway API 推理扩展（Inference）
lectures.topic: 讲解 endpoint-picker 子命令与 InferencePool 在图中的处理，理解 NGF 如何与 Gateway API Inference Extension 协作。
learning_goals: 掌握 endpoint-picker shim 服务器的作用
理解 InferencePool 在 graph 中的处理
了解 NGINX 与外部 EPP 的交互（ext proc 头）
lectures.practice_task: 说明 endpoint-picker 如何把 NGINX 的请求转发给真正的 EndpointPicker 服务。

lectures.title: NGINX Plus 能力：动态上游、授权与用量上报
lectures.topic: 讲解 NGINX Plus 相对 OSS 的差异：动态 upstream 更新、Plus API/mgmt 配置、licensing 与 usage report。
learning_goals: 掌握 UpdateUpstreamServers 动态更新机制
理解 Plus mgmt/usage report 配置生成
了解 licensing deployment context 的收集
lectures.practice_task: 对比 OSS 与 Plus 在 upstream 更新路径上的差异，说明 Plus 为何能不 reload。

lectures.title: TLS 安全：前端证书、后端 TLS 与 Agent mTLS
lectures.topic: 讲解前端 TLS 终止、BackendTLSPolicy（后端 mTLS/CA）、以及控制面↔数据面 Agent 的 mTLS 安全。
learning_goals: 掌握前端 TLS 证书对的处理
理解 BackendTLSPolicy 在图中的解析
了解 Agent mTLS 证书的生成与使用
lectures.practice_task: 为一个 HTTPS 后端配置 BackendTLSPolicy，并追踪 CA bundle 如何进入 NGINX 配置。

lectures.title: SnippetsFilter 与认证过滤器
lectures.topic: 讲解 SnippetsFilter/SnippetsPolicy（注入 NGINX 配置片段）与 AuthenticationFilter（Basic/JWT/OIDC）两种扩展机制。
learning_goals: 掌握 snippets 的允许上下文与注入方式
理解认证过滤器的多种认证类型
了解这些扩展的安全约束与 Plus 依赖
lectures.practice_task: 为一个 HTTPRoute 通过 SnippetsFilter 注入一条自定义 NGINX 指令，并验证生效。

units.title: 测试、性能与二次开发

lectures.title: 测试体系：单元、Fake 与集成测试
lectures.topic: 讲解 NGF 的测试方法：counterfeiter 生成的 fake、envtest 集成测试、conformance 测试的组织。
learning_goals: 掌握 controllerfakes/statefakes 等 fake 的生成与使用
理解 controller_suite_test 的 envtest 集成测试
了解 Gateway API conformance 测试入口
lectures.practice_task: 为一个新函数编写单元测试，并使用现有 fake 替换依赖。

lectures.title: 并发、性能与可靠性设计
lectures.topic: 从架构视角讲解 NGF 的并发与可靠性：事件双缓冲、leader 选举、冲突排序（ngfsort）、健康检查与就绪。
learning_goals: 理解事件双缓冲对吞吐与 reload 次数的影响
掌握 leader 选举对一致性的保障
了解 ngfsort 在冲突解析中的作用
lectures.practice_task: 分析一次资源高频变更场景下，双缓冲如何降低 NGINX reload 次数。

lectures.title: 二次开发：新增功能与扩展点
lectures.topic: 结合开发文档，讲解如何为 NGF 新增一个功能（如新策略/新 CRD），贯穿 API→图→配置生成→测试的完整改动。
learning_goals: 掌握新增 CRD/策略的完整改动链路
理解 implementing-a-feature 的开发规范
了解代码生成（generate-all）与 PR 流程
lectures.practice_task: 设计一个新的简单策略 CRD，列出从 API 定义到配置生成需要改动的文件清单。
